special_rules.name: Special Rules
special_rules.text: The They Shall Know No Fear rule applies to all Grey Knights formations except Inquisitorial forces (see They Shall Know No Fear).

special_rules.name: Grey Knights Transport
special_rules.text:
The Grey Knights are a highly mobile army. Because of this, the points cost of a detachment usually includes enough Rhino transport vehicles to transport it and any upgrades that have been taken. Determine the number of Rhinos needed after all upgrades have been purchased. The number of Rhinos will always be the minimum needed to carry the formation; you can’t take extras along to cover any losses.

Note that many formations don’t receive Rhinos, usually because they can’t fit into them. Detachments that come with Rhinos will be noted as having plus transport in the units section of the army list.

Also note that you don’t have to take Rhinos if you don’t want to. If you’d rather field the formation on foot instead, so it can act as a garrison for example, or be transported in a Thunderhawk Gunship, then you may do so.

In addition, you may choose to replace a detachment’s Rhinos with Drop Pods. If you do this then the detachment will enter play in a Drop Pod using the rules for Planetfall. Note that if you choose to do this you will also require at least one Space Marine Strike Cruiser or Battle Barge to deploy the Drop Pods from.

special_rules.name: They Shall Know No Fear
special_rules.text:
Grey Knights are renowned for their tenacity and bravery. This is represented by the following rules:

- It takes 2 Blast markers to suppress a Grey Knight unit or kill a unit in a broken formation (ignore any leftover Blast markers).
- Grey Knight formations are only broken if they have 2 Blast markers per unit in the formation.
- Grey Knight formations only count half their number of Blast markers in assault resolution (rounding down; note that assault resolution will not receive +1 for having no Blast markers if the formation has 1 Blast marker before rounding down). Halve the number of extra hits suffered by a Grey Knight formation that loses an assault, rounding down in favour of the Grey Knights.
- When a broken Grey Knight formation rallies, it receives a number of Blast markers equal to the number of units, rather than half this number. Grey Knights with the Leader special ability remove 2 Blast markers instead of 1.

special_rules.name: Into the Maw of the Beast
special_rules.text: Grey Knight battle doctrine frequently requires rapid insertion into the heart of battle where the fighting is the thickest to subdue a demon incursion. Because of this experience combined with their access to greater technology over the standard Adeptus Astartes chapter, units with Teleport may choose to enter the battlefield using Self-Planetfall. Announce the choice at the beginning of the game and plot accordingly per the rules of Self-Planetfall.

profiles.name: Death Cult Assassin
parse_warnings: The source abbreviates one Death Cult Assassin weapon trait as 'S'; preserved as printed because the intended expansion is not completely clear in context.
ambiguity_reasons: Compressed weapon-trait abbreviation in the PDF reference sheet.

profiles.name: Chimera
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May carry 2 Inquisitorial Warrior Acolytes and/or Death Cult Assassin units.

profiles.name: Grey Knight Razorback
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May carry 1 of the following Grey Knights units: Purgation Squad, Strike Squad, Purifier Squad.

profiles.name: Grey Knight Dreadnought
abilities_or_notes: Counts as Dreadnought for transport purposes.
parse_warnings: This profile includes mutually exclusive Grey Knight Dreadnought loadouts: either 2x TL Auto Cannon or Doom Fist and Heavy Psycannon.

profiles.name: Land Raider
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport one Terminator unit or two infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted.
Counts as Land Raider for transport purposes.

profiles.name: Land Raider Crusader
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport two Terminator units or three infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted.
Counts as Land Raider for transport purposes.

profiles.name: Land Raider Redeemer
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport one Terminator unit or two infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted.
Counts as Land Raider for transport purposes.

profiles.name: Nemesis Dreadknight
abilities_or_notes: Counts as Dreadnought for transport purposes.

profiles.name: Rhino
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May carry 2 of the following: Purgation Squad, Strike Squad, Purifier Squad, Inquisitorial Storm Trooper.

profiles.name: Stormraven Gunship
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May carry 1 Grey Knights Dreadnought or 2 of the following: Interceptor, Purgation Squad, Strike Squad, Purifier Squad.

profiles.name: Valkyrie
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport two infantry units without Jump Packs or Mounted.
parse_warnings: The Valkyrie Rocket Pod line uses the source abbreviation 'SS'; preserved as printed because the intended expansion is not explicitly stated on the page.
ambiguity_reasons: Compressed weapon-trait abbreviation in the PDF reference sheet.

profiles.name: Thunderhawk Gunship
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport eight infantry units, Attack Bikes or Dreadnoughts; Terminator units and Dreadnoughts count as two units each.
Critical Hit Effect: The unit and all units being carried are destroyed.

profiles.name: Landing Craft
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport twelve infantry units or Dreadnoughts; Terminator units and Dreadnoughts count as two units each; plus any six armoured vehicle units (except Dreadnoughts); Land Raiders count as one and a half units each, rounding up.
Critical Hit Effect: The unit and all units being carried are destroyed. All units within 5cm suffer a hit.

profiles.name: Thunderhawk Transporter
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May carry 1 Land Raider or 2 of the following units: Hunter, Nemesis Dreadknight, Razorback, Rhino, plus any infantry units carried in the transported vehicles.
Critical Hit Effect: The unit and all units being carried are destroyed.

profiles.name: Grey Knights Drop Pod
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport one formation of only the following units: Purgation Squad, Strike Squad, Purifier Squad, Dreadnought or Nemesis Dreadknight.
After the Drop Pod lands, its Deathwind attacks all enemy units within 15cm. Each enemy formation attacked receives a Blast marker for coming under fire, and an extra Blast marker for each casualty.

Then any troops carried in the Drop Pod must disembark within 5cm of the Drop Pod or within 5cm of another unit from the same formation that has already landed, so long as all units are placed within 15cm of the Drop Pod.
Drop Pod models should be removed from the board once the formation they transport has disembarked.

profiles.name: Strike Cruiser
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport 20 infantry units, Attack Bikes, or Dreadnoughts; plus 20 light vehicles or armoured vehicle units (except Attack Bikes and Dreadnoughts); plus six Thunderhawk Gunships and enough Drop Pods, or Landing Craft to transport any other units being carried.

profiles.name: Battle Barge
abilities_or_notes: Transport: May transport 60 infantry units, Attack Bikes, or Dreadnoughts; plus 60 armoured vehicle units (except Dreadnoughts); plus nine Thunderhawk Gunships and enough Drop Pods or Landing Craft to transport any other units being carried.

army_notes: A Grey Knight army must contain at least one Strike Force.
Each Core Formation unlocks one Elite Formation, two Grey Knight Support Formations, and two Inquisition Support Formations.
A Grey Knight army may take one Elite Formation for each Core Formation.
Up to two Grey Knight Support formations may be taken for each Core Formation.
Up to two Inquisition Support formations may be taken for each Core Formation.
Grey Knight Aircraft total cost cannot exceed one third of total army cost.
Up to three upgrades may be taken per Grey Knight formation.
Each upgrade may be taken once per formation unless otherwise noted.
Inquisitorial forces do not benefit from They Shall Know No Fear.
extraction_warnings: Used existing repo list id INQ_gk2018_NETEA.
The Eternal Warrior Formation source row was split into two formation entries to preserve the two printed alternatives and their different costs.
Some profile abbreviations were preserved conservatively where the PDF was compressed: Death Cult Assassin weapon trait 'S' and Valkyrie Rocket Pod trait 'SS'.
The formation name 'Inquisitional Tank Company' from extracted PDF text was normalized to 'Inquisitorial Tank Company'.
